label: Operations / HR / People Ops

categories.label: Quantified People-Ops Impact
categories.rubric: Score 0-40 on % of bullets with volume/range metrics (resumes screened, interviews scheduled, hires supported, onboarding docs, events coordinated, attendance). 'Assisted with recruiting' = 0; 'Screened 240+ resumes, coordinated 38 interview panels, built onboarding tracker adopted by 3 teams' = strong. Ops/HR rewards scale and process rigor.

categories.label: HR / Ops Domain Fit
categories.rubric: Score 0-25 on HR/people-ops/ops-program relevance. Strong: prior HR/people-ops/recruiting internship, SHRM student chapter officer, event coordination at scale, admin/RA/customer-service work framed as people-facing. Weak: purely solo/technical background with no team contact.

categories.label: Tools & Systems
categories.rubric: Score 0-15 on named tools actually used. Exact: ATS (Greenhouse, Lever, Jobvite, Taleo), HRIS (Workday, BambooHR, ADP, SAP SuccessFactors), LinkedIn Recruiter / Boolean search. Related: Excel/Sheets, PowerPoint, Tableau, Asana/Smartsheet. Penalize 'familiar with' padding on tools never actually used.

categories.label: Format / ATS
categories.rubric: Score 0-10 on 1-page rule, clean sections, no tables/columns, .docx + .pdf export tested, consistent dates. HR screens hundreds of resumes per req — whitespace and scannability matter.

categories.label: Discretion & Brand Signals
categories.rubric: Score 0-10 on LinkedIn headline + experience matching resume, plus explicit discretion/confidentiality framing on roles that handled sensitive data. HR/recruiting requires trust signals — show you understand confidentiality, not just claim it.

categories.label: Extras & Process Rigor
categories.rubric: Score 0-10 on SHRM student chapter membership, aPHR / SHRM-CP path, LinkedIn Learning / Coursera HR certs, peer mentoring / tutoring, DEI initiative involvement, AI/automation fluency for HR workflows, bias-for-action anecdotes (built a tracker unprompted, fixed a process).

skills.name: Compliance basics (I-9, FMLA, EEO, labor law)
Excel / Google Sheets (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, formulas)

bonus_signals: People-facing role at scale (RA, customer service, front desk, hospitality) with volume metrics
SHRM student chapter leadership (president, officer, events lead)
Event coordination at scale (career fairs, recruiting days) with headcount metrics
Named ATS/HRIS hands-on experience (Workday, Greenhouse, Lever, BambooHR)
SHRM / HRCI student membership or aPHR path
AI / automation fluency for HR workflows
Bias-for-action anecdotes (built a tracker unprompted, fixed a process)
